methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $271M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Path Robotics, a Series D robotics and manufacturing platform founded by Andy and Alex Lonsberry, is evaluated against a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable. The private peer set includes Exotec, Divergent, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics, spanning Series C to Series E stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using $271M in total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yields $406M. The peer-median method applies the median valuation of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—$1.8B, with a range of $1.0B to $2.3B—to establish an upper anchor point. This reflects valuations of Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $346M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding Path Robotics' relative market position, revenue scale, and unit economics compared to the peer set. The stage-progression floor assumes limited value creation since Series A; the peer-median ceiling assumes path to comparable scale. Resolution of this range would require clarity on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and near-term capital efficiency.
